claim.statement: Deliberately slow down actions with financial, legal, safety, or emergency consequences (transfers, payments, signing agreements, dispatching emergency services). Add a review/confirmation step that summarises amounts, parties, and effects, and avoid one-tap execution or optimistic shortcuts for these actions.

limitations: Not backed by a single authoritative standard; appropriate friction is domain- and regulation-specific. Treated as a hypothesis to validate per context

do: Add an explicit review screen summarising amount, recipient/parties, and effect
Require deliberate confirmation rather than implicit/one-tap execution
Surface irreversibility and timing (e.g. when a transfer settles)
avoid: One-tap or optimistic execution of money-moving, legal, or emergency actions
Burying critical figures or parties below the fold of the confirm step

strategies: remove one-tap shortcuts for high-stakes actions
